https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/actions/runs/23697472039/job/69035052597?pr=384 the latest web e2e test results are not in, but it seems like the release gate is not requiring any e2e test run for these latest changes. most likely its because nothing in the web app changed, but the updated tests should have set the flag to require release gate checks.
user_feedback_text: https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/actions/runs/23697472039/job/69035052597?pr=384 the latest web e2e test results are not in, but it seems like the release gate is not requiring any e2e test run for these latest changes. most likely its because nothing in the web app changed, but the updated tests should have set the flag to require release gate checks.

task:
the e2e run was correctly kicked off after the pr to main was merged https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/actions/runs/23712125460

the issue here is that the release please release gate did not block merging until the web e2e test run was finished.
user_feedback_text:
the e2e run was correctly kicked off after the pr to main was merged https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/actions/runs/23712125460

the issue here is that the release please release gate did not block merging until the web e2e test run was finished.

task: [Request interrupted by user]

I think that the reason the release gate was not required is that the PR was the `test:` commit message when the PR was merged. I had to manually kick off the `Release gate job` to see that the e2e tests were failing.
user_feedback_text: I think that the reason the release gate was not required is that the PR was the `test:` commit message when the PR was merged. I had to manually kick off the `Release gate job` to see that the e2e tests were failing.

task: this is once again not the issue here - everything works, but since the `test(34):` PR was merged https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/commit/78bef4b897570574d0cf2997ec0f4a93ba0408c9 but was not included in the changelog of the next release, the verify-e2e should still have flagged the changes and required the e2e tests to pass before the release please pr was merged. this should have happened automatically
user_feedback_text: this is once again not the issue here - everything works, but since the `test(34):` PR was merged https://github.com/FSM1/cipher-box/commit/78bef4b897570574d0cf2997ec0f4a93ba0408c9 but was not included in the changelog of the next release, the verify-e2e should still have flagged the changes and required the e2e tests to pass before the release please pr was merged. this should have happened automatically

<command-name>/resolve-pr-reviews</command-name>

so currently, the web e2e always runs on merges to main while desktop-e2e runs on changes to the desktop app.

would it make sense to gate the web-e2e to only run when the web (or any of its dependencies) is updated?
user_feedback_text:
so currently, the web e2e always runs on merges to main while desktop-e2e runs on changes to the desktop app.

would it make sense to gate the web-e2e to only run when the web (or any of its dependencies) is updated?

task: Yes I think this is a great pattern to follow. while we are at it, would it possibly make sense to use once common `detect changes` job driving whether the `web-e2e` and `desktop-e2e` are needed
user_feedback_text: Yes I think this is a great pattern to follow. while we are at it, would it possibly make sense to use once common `detect changes` job driving whether the `web-e2e` and `desktop-e2e` are needed
